phoneticUs: ňĆőłiňźo äňąbro äňĆmiňźn; ňĆőłiňźo äňąbro äm…™n
examples: Theobromine appears to help with repairing enamel and protecting teeth from further erosion by acids.
Apart from being a stimulant, theobromine is also a vasodilator (widener of blood vessels) and a diuretic.
By being able to increase heartbeat, while dilating blood vessels, theobromine can help reduce high blood pressure.
Long sleep was associated with consuming less theobromine, which is found in chocolate and tea, the saturated fat dodecanoic acid, choline found in eggs and fatty meats and total carbohydrates.
